content.problemStatement:
Chef has a memory machine. It has one layer for data storage and another layer for cache. Chef has stored an array with length NNN in the first layer; let's denote its elements by A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AN‚àí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0‚Äã,A1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚àí1‚Äã. Now he wants to load some elements of this array into the cache.

The machine loads the array in blocks with size BBB: A0,A1,‚Ä¶,AB‚àí1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{B-1}A0‚Äã,A1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AB‚àí1‚Äã form a block, AB,AB+1,‚Ä¶,A2B‚àí1A_B, A_{B+1}, \ldots, A_{2B-1}AB‚Äã,AB+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,A2B‚àí1‚Äã form another block, and so on. The last block may contain less than BBB elements of Chef's array. The cache may only contain at most one block at a time. Whenever Chef tries to access an element AiA_iAi‚Äã, the machine checks if the block where AiA_iAi‚Äã is located is already in the cache, and if it is not, loads this block into the cache layer, so that it can quickly access any data in it. However, as soon as Chef tries to access any element that is outside the block currently loaded in the cache, the block that was previously loaded into the cache is removed from the cache, since the machine loads a new block containing the element that is being accessed.

Chef has a sequence of elements Ax1,Ax2,‚Ä¶,AxMA_{x_1}, A_{x_2}, \ldots, A_{x_M}Ax1‚Äã‚Äã,Ax2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AxM‚Äã‚Äã which he wants to access, in this order. Initially, the cache is empty. Chef is wondering how many times the machine will need to load a block into the cache layer. Can you help him calculate this number?
constraints: 0‚â§xi<N0 \le x_i \lt N0‚â§xi‚Äã<N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, BBB and MMM.
The second line contains MMM space-separated integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xMx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Mx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xM‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of times the machine loads a block into the cache.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The machine stores elements [A0,A1,A2][A_0, A_1, A_2][A0‚Äã,A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã] in one block and [A3,A4][A_3, A_4][A3‚Äã,A4‚Äã] in another block. When accessing A0A_0A0‚Äã, the block [A0,A1,A2][A_0, A_1, A_2][A0‚Äã,A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã] is loaded. Then, accessing A3A_3A3‚Äã removes the previous block from the cache and loads the block [A3,A4][A_3, A_4][A3‚Äã,A4‚Äã]. Finally, when Chef accesses A4A_4A4‚Äã, a new block is not loaded, since the block containing A4A_4A4‚Äã is currently loaded in the cache.
